data:
Process Documentation Report · Single-Site Underground Gold Mine · Finance Department

Finance Department Process Documentation & Flow Synthesis

Underground Gold Mining Operation — ~600 employees on site — Finance function: ~20 staff
Three detailed interviews (David Motswari, Priya Desai, James Kipchoge) reveal a finance function that is operationally effective due to individual competence and informal controls, but structurally fragile. Key findings: heavy reliance on spreadsheet workarounds bridging disconnected source systems (Ellipse, MineRP, Microster, Aspen), significant key-person dependencies concentrated in three individuals, and a recurring pattern of upstream coding errors that propagate uncorrected through the transaction chain. The function is well-positioned for targeted automation and process improvement.
🎙️
3
Interviews completed
🗂️
14
Process domains in scope
⚡
30–60
Recode journals per month
👤
3
Critical key-person dependencies

1 Finance Function Overview — Interviewees & Team Structure

DM

David Motswari

Business Partner Finance, Underground Ops
On-Site Business Partner
Tenure~12 years (Senior)
AreaUnderground Development & Production
FocusCost tracking, variance commentary, accruals, business cases, contractor verification
PD

Priya Desai

AP & Procurement Coordinator
Central Finance
TenureMid-level (experienced)
AreaAll operational areas — AP & Procurement
FocusInvoice processing, 3-way match, payment runs, PO coordination, vendor management
JK

James Kipchoge

Finance Systems Admin & Analyst
Central Finance
TenureMid-to-Senior
AreaCross-functional systems support
FocusEllipse ERP admin, Cognos reporting, data extraction, system design
Non-interviewed staff referenced: Margaret (Finance Manager/Controller — approves journals, oversees close, reviews business cases), Amara (Management Accountant — cost allocation, recode journals), Themba (Section Engineer — intended GR confirmer), Johan (Operations Manager — expenditure approvals). Priority: Margaret and Amara should be next interviews.

2 Finance Team Structure

Finance Manager / Controller"] subgraph Central["Central Finance Team"] direction TB PD["🟣 Priya Desai
AP & Procurement Coordinator"] AM["Amara
Management Accountant"] JK["🟢 James Kipchoge
Systems Admin & Analyst"] PA["Payroll Administrator"] RA["Revenue / AR Accountant"] FA["Fixed Assets Accountant"] TC["Tax & Compliance Officer"] end subgraph SiteBP["On-Site Business Partners"] direction TB DM["🔵 David Motswari
Underground Ops BP"] PBP["Plant Processing BP"] EBP["Engineering & Maintenance BP"] HSEBP["HSE Support"] end FM --> Central FM --> SiteBP DM -- "Cost data, accrual calls,
contractor verification" --> FM PD -- "Payment batches,
AP aging" --> FM AM -- "Recode journals,
cost allocation" --> FM style FM fill:#1e293b,color:white,stroke:#1e293b style Central fill:#eff6ff,stroke:#2563eb style SiteBP fill:#f0fdf4,stroke:#059669 style DM fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb style PD fill:#ede9fe,stroke:#7c3aed style JK fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669
Finance function splits into centralized transactional/reporting staff and operationally embedded business partners. Coordination between the two sides is predominantly informal — phone, email, and in-person — with no structured handoff protocols.

3 Systems Landscape & Integration Gaps

Flat-file / partial integration
No integration — manual workaround
Core ERP (Ellipse)
Reporting layer
No integration
Flat-file only
Pervasive workaround
Ellipse (ERP) is the intended system of record but is isolated from all four key operational data sources. Excel acts as the connective tissue across every integration gap — a fragile architecture with high key-person dependency and version control risk.

4 Process Domain Coverage & Documentation Status

Process Domain Coverage Status Key Roles Documented Critical Gaps
Six of fourteen domains are well or partially documented from three interviews. Four domains (AR/Revenue, Tax, Intercompany, and meaningful coverage of Cash Management) are entirely absent and require dedicated interview sessions.

5 Key Process Flows

flowchart TD A([📩 Invoice arrives in shared AP inbox]) --> B["1. Sort & classify\nPriya — Outlook"] B --> C{PO number\non invoice?} C -->|Yes| D["2. Locate PO in Ellipse\nPriya"] C -->|No 35% of cases| E["2a. Detective work:\nSearch Ellipse by vendor/date\nChase originating dept — Email"] E --> |"PO found?"| D E -->|Not found| PARK["🔴 Park: 'Pending - Awaiting PO'\nLog in tracking spreadsheet\nWait for department response"] PARK -.->|"PO eventually raised"| D D --> F{"Goods receipt\nconfirmed in Ellipse?"} F -->|Yes| MATCH["4. Three-way match:\nCompare PO + GR + Invoice\nPriya — Manual PDF vs screen"] F -->|No — 2-3 day typical delay| G["3a. Chase ops staff\nby email/phone"] F -->|Contractor invoices| H["3b. Phone David\nif Themba unavailable"] G --> F H -->|"David confirms by email"| GR["Receipt goods in Ellipse"] GR --> MATCH MATCH --> TOL{"Within\ntolerance?"} TOL -->|Yes| POST["5. Code & post invoice\nEllipse GL"] TOL -->|No| DISC["Investigate discrepancy\nwith vendor or requester"] DISC --> MATCH POST --> APPR{"ERP approval\nnotification working?"} APPR -->|Yes| ERPA["6a. ERP approval workflow\nDept head / Johan / Margaret"] APPR -->|No 'unreliable'| PAPER["6b. Print invoice + PO summary\nPhysical delivery to approver\nParallel paper pack process"] ERPA --> PAY PAPER -->|"Signed pack returned"| PAY["8. Wednesday PM:\nPrepare payment run\nPriya — Ellipse + Excel bank list"] PAY --> AUTH["9. Margaret reviews &\nauthorizes payment batch\nEllipse"] AUTH --> REM["10. Send remittance\nadvices to vendors\nEmail — vendor-specific format"] REM --> END([✅ Payment cycle complete]) style A fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb style END fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669 style PARK fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626 style PAPER fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706 style H fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706
~50–70 invoices per week. Three structural weaknesses: (1) ~35% require investigation due to absent POs; (2) ERP approval notifications are unreliable, forcing a parallel paper process; (3) contractor verification routes informally through David, creating a segregation-of-duties gap.
flowchart TD BLAST([⛏️ Development blast occurs on shift]) --> EXP["Explosives issued from magazine\nCost booked immediately on issue\nEllipse Inventory — regardless of blast outcome"] EXP --> SURV["Survey dept measures advance\nafter cleanup\nMineRP — ~1 week lag"] SURV --> CSV["Monthly CSV extract of\nsurvey metres\nMineRP → Email to David & mine planning"] CSV --> TRACKER["David manually enters\nsurvey metres into tracker\nExcel — Survey Reconciliation tab"] TRACKER --> RECON{"Costs and metres\naligned?"} RECON -->|Yes| DASH["Update tracker dashboard:\nCost per metre\nExcel — Dashboard tab"] RECON -->|Gap detected| INVEST["Investigate discrepancy:\nReview survey report\nVisit underground\nPhone shift boss\nJudgment — four inputs"] INVEST --> ACCRUAL{"Timing issue\nor real cost\noverrun?"} ACCRUAL -->|Judgment call| CALL["Phone Margaret\nto agree accrual treatment\nNo documented protocol"] CALL --> JRNL["Margaret's team posts\nmanual accrual journal\nEllipse — DR cost centre\nCR accrual account"] JRNL --> DASH DASH --> RAG["Set RAG status\nManual judgment\n~15% threshold rule — informal\nExcel — color coded"] RAG --> COMM["Write monthly variance\ncommentary per cost centre\nDavid — Word document"] COMM --> DIST["Email to Margaret & Johan\nCopy: local machine\n+ lever arch file\nNO shared system storage"] DIST --> END([📊 Monthly underground cost report complete]) style BLAST fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb style END fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669 style DIST fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626 style CALL fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706 style RAG fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706
The entire underground cost intelligence layer depends on David's Excel tracker — four years of data residing on a single laptop with no shared access or backup protocol. The survey-to-cost reconciliation is manual and lag-affected every month.
flowchart TD TRIG([📅 Month-end cost report review triggered]) --> EXPORT["Amara exports cost centre\nreports from Ellipse/Cognos\nExcel — manual export"] EXPORT --> REVIEW["Review each cost centre\nfor anomalies\nAmara — pattern recognition\nno automated detection"] REVIEW --> FOUND{"Suspected\nmiscode\nidentified?"} FOUND -->|No| PASS["Accept and proceed\nto allocations"] FOUND -->|Yes 30-60 per month| DRILL["Drill into source transaction\nin Ellipse: PO / work order /\noriginal requisition\nAmara — Ellipse"] DRILL --> CONFIRM{"Error\nconfirmed?"} CONFIRM -->|No| PASS CONFIRM -->|Yes| CHASE["Chase originator\nfor context if needed\nEmail / conversation"] CHASE --> PREP["Prepare recode journal\nin spreadsheet template\nDebit correct CC\nCredit incorrect CC\nAmara — Excel template"] PREP --> ENTRY["Enter journal into Ellipse\nAmara or James if needed"] ENTRY --> THRESHOLD{"Above\napproval\nthreshold?"} THRESHOLD -->|Yes| MARGARET["Margaret reviews &\napproves journal\nEllipse"] THRESHOLD -->|Below threshold| POSTED MARGARET --> POSTED["Journal posted to ledger"] POSTED --> GAP["🔴 No notification to originator\nFree-text narrative field\ninconsistently populated\nError likely to recur"] GAP --> END([Month-end ledger corrected]) style TRIG fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb style END fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669 style GAP fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626 style PREP fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706
30–60 recode journals per month represent a structural quality failure, not a normal operating cost. The root cause is upstream coding at requisition stage with no finance validation. No feedback loop to originators means the behavior that creates errors is never corrected. James Kipchoge: "The correction fixes the ledger. It doesn't fix the process that caused the error."
flowchart TD T([⏱️ Weekly time & attendance cycle — Microster]) --> EXTRACT["Weekly flat-file extract\ngenerated by HR\nEmployee ID, hours,\nshift type, HR dept code"] EXTRACT --> MAP["Apply mapping table:\nTranslate HR dept codes\nto finance cost centres\nExcel — dual-version\nspreadsheet RISK"] MAP --> MAPCHECK{"All HR codes\nhave a mapping?"} MAPCHECK -->|Yes| REVIEW["Review extract for\nanomalies — extent of\njudgment unknown\nFinance — Excel"] MAPCHECK -->|New/changed org code| MANUAL["Manual investigation\nrequired — no protocol"] MANUAL --> REVIEW REVIEW --> IMPORT["Import / journal labour\ncosts into Ellipse\nby cost centre"] IMPORT --> VALIDATION{"Import\nvalidation?"} VALIDATION -->|"🔴 None — posts silently"| POSTED["Labour costs posted\nto GL cost centres\nNO error flagging"] VALIDATION -->|Should be| NEVER["Never implemented"] POSTED --> MONTHEND["Month-end: Amara reviews\ncost centre reports\npattern recognition only"] MONTHEND --> DETECT{"Anomaly\ndetected?"} DETECT -->|Yes| RECODE["Recode journal process\n→ See Recode flow"] DETECT -->|No| PERSIST["🔴 Error persists\nin reported costs"] PERSIST --> END([Labour costs in management accounts]) RECODE --> END style T fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#2563eb style END fill:#d1fae5,stroke:#059669 style POSTED fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626 style PERSIST fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626 style MAP fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#d97706 style VALIDATION fill:#fee2e2,stroke:#dc2626
Labour is the largest single cost category — yet it has the weakest systemic controls. Two versions of the HR-to-finance mapping table exist with no authoritative owner, and there is zero import validation. Errors post silently and detection relies entirely on one person's pattern recognition at month-end.

6 Cross-Process Observations

⚙️ System Integration

No Integration Between Operational Systems and Ellipse ERP

MineRP (survey), Aspen (plant), Microster (time & attendance), and Deswik (planning) all feed into finance processes but none have live or automated integration with Ellipse. Every data transfer is manual — CSV files, flat-file extracts, or re-keying.

"It works because people fill the gap. That's the risk." — David Motswari
🔄 Handoff Friction

Goods Receipt Confirmation Bottleneck

2–3 day typical delay for operational staff to confirm goods receipts in Ellipse. This cascades into missed payment runs, vendor relationship strain, and Priya conducting informal verifications. Intended control path (via Themba) is routinely bypassed.

"There's no consequence for not raising a PO… so the behaviour doesn't change." — Priya Desai
📊 Data Quality

Upstream Coding Errors Propagate Uncorrected

Cost coding errors at requisition stage (performed by operational staff with no finance review) propagate through PO → goods receipt → invoice → ledger. Detection only occurs at month-end via manual review. 30–60 recode journals monthly = direct evidence of structural failure.

"The correction fixes the ledger. It doesn't fix the process." — James Kipchoge
👤 Key-Person Risk

Critical Knowledge Concentrated in Three Individuals

David's tracker (4 years on one laptop), Amara's allocation spreadsheet (sole maintainer), and Priya's compensating controls (undocumented) each represent single points of failure. No documented succession, no shared system access, no backup process for any of these.

"If I got hit by a truck tomorrow, they'd be in trouble. I know that." — David Motswari
⚙️ ERP Workflow Failure

Unreliable ERP Approval Notifications Force Parallel Process

Ellipse approval notifications are unreliable enough that Priya maintains a full parallel paper-based approval process — printing invoice packs, physically walking them to approvers, and filing signed copies. The ERP workflow exists but cannot be relied upon for deadline-critical processing.

"I can't rely on it alone if I have a deadline. So I have the parallel process as backup." — Priya Desai
📊 Labour Cost Risk

HR-to-Finance Mapping Table Has No Authoritative Version

Two teams (finance and HR) maintain separate copies of the cost centre mapping table. No formal protocol for updates on org changes. No import validation in Ellipse. If the mapping is wrong, labour costs post silently to incorrect cost centres. Labour is the largest single cost category.

All six cross-process observations are addressable with targeted interventions — several (structured journal narratives, mapping table governance, ERP notification fix) are low-cost and could be implemented quickly without system replacement.

7 Key-Person Dependency Analysis

⚠ CRITICAL
DM

David Motswari

Business Partner — Underground Operations
  • Excel tracker (4 years history) — laptop only, no backup
  • All underground variance commentary — Word docs in email + physical file
  • Accrual judgment calls — informal phone protocol with Margaret
  • Contractor receipt verification — informal bypass when Themba unavailable
  • All business case institutional knowledge and approvals in Outlook folder
  • RAG threshold rule (~15%) — unformalized, in his head
"If I got hit by a truck tomorrow, they'd be in trouble. I know that."
⚠ CRITICAL
AM

Amara

Management Accountant — Central Finance
  • Sole maintainer of mining-to-processing cost allocation spreadsheet
  • Allocation drivers and their currency unknown to any other team member
  • Primary executor of 30–60 recode journals monthly
  • Pattern recognition for labour cost anomalies at month-end
  • Allocation methodology not documented anywhere
Described by James Kipchoge as a single point of failure for the entire cost allocation process.
↑ HIGH
PD

Priya Desai

AP & Procurement Coordinator — Central Finance
  • AP tracking spreadsheet — compensating control not in ERP
  • Vendor preference list (bank details, contacts) — informal personal record
  • Bank detail verification process — undocumented personal practice
  • Paper pack approval system — full parallel process not documented
  • Vendor relationship knowledge — who to call, escalation paths
All compensating controls that exist only because Priya built them — none are documented or systematized.
Key-person risk is not limited to David — it is systemic. Each of three interviewed individuals maintains critical undocumented controls. Amara (not yet interviewed) likely carries equal or greater risk given her sole ownership of the cost allocation model.

8 Manual Effort Intensity by Process Domain

Manual effort scoring is based on interview evidence: frequency × degree of manual intervention per cycle. Accounts Payable (3-way match volume), Labour Cost Posting (mapping + validation gaps), and Management Reporting (Excel-dependent) show the highest automation opportunity.

9 Interview Gap Assessment & Priority Next Steps

🔴 Priority 1 — Critical Gaps

AR & Revenue Recognition — gold doré sales, refinery settlements, revenue posting — zero documentation.

Tax & Statutory Compliance — entire process undocumented; regulatory risk if not understood.

Amara (Management Accountant) — sole owner of cost allocation model, primary recode journal processor — must be interviewed to understand allocation methodology and month-end close detail.

🟡 Priority 2 — Significant Gaps

Margaret (Finance Manager) — owns the full close process, approval authority, reporting pack — her perspective needed for month-end close, year-end, and compliance processes.

Payroll Administrator — the person actually performing the weekly Microster-to-Ellipse process is unknown; James only has system-level observation, not operational detail.

🔵 Priority 3 — Partial Coverage

Budgeting & Forecasting — only referenced in passing; full cycle, tools, and participants undocumented.

Fixed Assets & CapEx — business case process documented but asset register, depreciation, WIP capitalization not covered.

Month-End & Year-End Close — checklist, full timeline, and reconciliation procedures missing.

🟢 Priority 4 — Confirm & Validate

Intercompany & Recharges — may not apply to single-site; confirm with Margaret.

Inventory & Stores Finance Interface — physical count, obsolescence, and reconciliation processes referenced but not detailed.

Cash Management — payment runs documented; bank reconciliation, cash forecasting, and cash position monitoring still needed.

At minimum four additional interviews (Amara, Margaret, Payroll Administrator, Revenue/AR Accountant) are required before the full process map can be considered complete. The Amara interview is most urgent given her single-point-of-failure status.

10 Automation & AI Opportunity Indicators

Opportunity scores are qualitative assessments based on process characteristics observed across interviews: volume/frequency (high = more automatable), rules-based nature (high = RPA suitable), data availability (high = AI-ready), and current manual burden. Accounts Payable and Labour Cost Posting are the highest-priority candidates for near-term automation.
Finance Process Documentation Report · Single-Site Underground Gold Mine · Three interviews completed of ~20 finance staff · Report basis: David Motswari, Priya Desai, James Kipchoge
